title: Phase 1B: wire ferray-linalg eig/lstsq/lu/etc into ferrotorch-core::linalg + cuSOLVER GPU dispatch
description:
Expand ferrotorch-core::linalg from 8 to ~33 functions by wrapping the new ferray-linalg APIs, AND add GPU dispatch via ferrotorch-gpu::cusolver. This is the single largest API gap in ferrotorch-core per docs/audit/01-ferrotorch-core.md.

Functions to add (all already exist in ferray-linalg per audit doc 16):
- eig, eigvals, eigh, eigvalsh (+ batched variants)
- lstsq
- lu, lu_factor, lu_solve
- slogdet
- vector_norm, multi_dot
- solve_triangular, matrix_power, matrix_exp, matrix_rank
- cross, diagonal
- svdvals
- tensorsolve, tensorinv
- cholesky_ex, inv_ex, solve_ex (status-returning variants)

Scope:
- Wrap each ferray-linalg fn as Tensor<T> -> Tensor<T> in ferrotorch-core/src/linalg.rs, mirroring existing svd/solve/det/inv/qr/cholesky pattern.
- Re-export from lib.rs.
- Add round-trip tests (e.g. eig -> reconstruction error, lu*solve == matmul^-1, etc.).
- Wire autograd grad_fns where applicable (eig backward, lu backward, lstsq backward — non-trivial; if deferred, document and create follow-up).

GPU discipline (CRITICAL — apply /rust-gpu-discipline before claiming done):
- ferrotorch-gpu HAS a cusolver.rs module already. cuSOLVER provides eig (gesvd-style), lstsq (gels), lu (getrf/getrs), cholesky variants. Use these for CUDA tensors, NOT a CPU bounce.
- Extend the GpuBackend trait in gpu_dispatch.rs with new methods: eig_f32/f64, eigh_f32/f64, lstsq_f32/f64, lu_factor_f32/f64, lu_solve_f32/f64. Default impls return FerrotorchError::Unsupported.
- Implement those methods in ferrotorch-gpu/src/cusolver.rs (or a new linalg dispatch file) calling cusolverDn* functions via cudarc. Run actual CUDA work; do NOT call cpu_to_gpu/gpu_to_cpu/ferray_linalg::eig/cpu_to_gpu — that is the forbidden pattern.
- Add Device::Cuda(_) match arms in core::linalg fn bodies that call self.gpu_backend().eig_f32(buf) — same shape as existing matmul/softmax/gelu dispatch.
- Forbidden patterns to grep for before reporting done:
  - 'to(Device::Cpu)' inside any new linalg fn
  - 'tensor_to_cpu' followed by ferray_linalg call followed by 'cpu_to_gpu'
  - 'unimplemented!' / 'todo!' / 'CPU fallback' comments
- Write at least one test per new op that constructs a Cuda tensor and verifies the result matches the CPU path numerically AND that the result tensor.device() == Cuda(_) (not silently moved to CPU).
- f64 cuSOLVER coverage is partial in cudarc; document f64 paths that fall back with a warn-once log.

Closes ~75% of the audit-flagged linalg gap. Big enabler for scientific computing workloads.
